commit.message:
docs: improve README with detailed architecture and third-party integrations

- Add comprehensive External Integrations section
- Add detailed system architecture diagram showing all layers
- Add integration overview diagram with data flow
- Document all 5 third-party services with:
  - Geyser (Yellowstone gRPC) - real-time streaming
  - Helius API - historical backfill
  - Metaplex JS SDK - NFT metadata
  - MongoDB - persistence
  - Elysia.js - HTTP server
- For each integration document:
  - Purpose and communication method
  - How it works with diagrams
  - API usage examples
  - Data flow explanation
- Add Transaction Lifecycle diagram
- Add Library Usage section with code examples
- Improve navigation with new table of contents
- Add emoji indicators for third-party services

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: refactor indexer as importable library

- Add public library API via src/lib/index.ts
- Export all services, types, and factory functions
- Configure package.json with exports field for library usage
- Add files field to control published files
- Add tsconfig.lib.json for library-specific build
- Create .npmignore to exclude source and dev files
- Refactor services to support both singleton and direct instantiation
- Make MetadataService constructor public
- Make ProcessorService accept options object
- Add BackfillConfig interface for configuration
- Update GeyserStreamingService with comprehensive JSDoc
- Add build:lib and build:all npm scripts
- Support dual-mode: standalone app and importable library

Library consumers can now:
import { GeyserStreamingService, MetadataService, ProcessorService } from 'solana-nft-indexer';
import type { Transaction, NFTMetadata } from 'solana-nft-indexer';

animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable npm trusted publishing and fix hardlink conversion (#26)

* feat: update Node.js version to 24 in workflows and configuration

* fix: hook convert-hardlink to package:js instead of non-existent release:npm

The convert-hardlink task was prepended to release:npm which does not
exist as a projen task, so hard links were never converted before
npm publish. This caused E415 "Hard link is not allowed" errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix RAPIDS base image tag: 24.12-cuda12.5-py3.11 (driver 580 / L40S compatible)

25.02-cuda12.6 tag doesn't exist on NVIDIA registry. 24.12-cuda12.5-py3.11
is verified available and supports driver 560+ (works with our driver 580).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add comprehensive AI in ASIC cookbook for ASIC engineers

864-line tutorial enabling ASIC designers to contribute to HiPilot:

- Chapter 1: The Big Picture - Why ASIC expertise matters for AI
- Chapter 2: MCP Demystified - Restaurant analogy for easy understanding
- Chapter 3: Skills - Teaching AI your expertise through markdown
- Chapter 4: Your First Skill - Step-by-step hold fixing skill creation
- Chapter 5: Templates - Tcl generation with variables and conditionals
- Chapter 6: Debugging - Tools and mindset for debugging AI
- Chapter 7: Recipes - Real-world examples (clock gating, DRC, MCMM)

Features:
- Vivid analogies (restaurant ordering system for MCP)
- Code examples with detailed comments
- ASIC-specific examples (useful skew, CTS, timing closure)
- Checklists for skills and templates
- Quick reference appendix

Target audience: ASIC engineers with no coding experience
Goal: Enable immediate contribution to HiPilot development

Add physics data (inertial + collision) to robot-car URDF

chassis: 150 g acrylic plate with box collision (220×150×3 mm)
wheels:  40 g each, cylinder collision (r=32.5 mm, w=15 mm)
caster:  10 g, cylinder collision (r=14.6 mm)

Enables MuJoCo simulation without tumbling — robot rests stably on
the injected floor plane with correct mass distribution.

feat(formal): close IDNA/Unicode/Lock gaps — K61-K68 (220 verification instances)

Closes the three remaining formal verification gaps that were previously
documented as "out of scope":

K61-K63 — IDNA domain normalization: normalize_domain_for_match extracted
with abstracted idna::domain_to_ascii as Result<String, ()> parameter.
Non-ASCII IDNA failure → None (fail-closed), ASCII fallback to lowercase,
wildcard prefix preserved through normalization.

K64-K65 — Unicode homoglyph normalization: normalize_homoglyphs verbatim
extraction (188-line char mapping covering Cyrillic, Greek, Armenian,
Cherokee, Fullwidth Latin, Palochka, dashes, curly quotes). Idempotency
proven (K64), all mapped confusables collapse to ASCII (K65).

K66-K68 — RwLock poisoning fail-closed: all 6 lock sites in engine/MCP
modeled as pure predicates. Cache → miss, Deputy → InternalError, Glob →
FreshCompile. No lock site ever produces stale Allow.

109 parity tests (up from 83). 220 total verification instances across 7
tools. All security-critical pure functions now formally verified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(rules): add rule to detect AWS accounts not synced (#2458)

### Type of change
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing
functionality to change)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Other (please describe):


### Summary

Adds a new security rule `aws_account_not_synced` that detects AWS
accounts present in the graph that are not being synced by Cartography.

The rule queries for `AWSAccount` nodes with 1 or fewer `RESOURCE`
relationships, which indicates the account was discovered (e.g. via AWS
Organizations) but its resources are not being ingested.

**Query logic:**
```cypher
MATCH (a:AWSAccount)
OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:RESOURCE]->(n)
WITH a, count(n) AS resource_count
WHERE resource_count <= 1
RETURN a.id AS account_id, a.name AS account_name, resource_count
```


### Related issues or links

- N/A


### How was this tested?

- Rule file follows existing patterns (e.g.
`cloud_security_product_deactivated.py`)
- Can be tested with `cartography-rules list aws_account_not_synced` and
`cartography-rules run aws_account_not_synced`


### Checklist

#### General
- [x] I have read the [contributing
guidelines](https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/dev/developer-guide.html).
- [ ] The linter passes locally (`make lint`).
- [ ] I have added/updated tests that prove my fix is effective or my
feature works.

#### Proof of functionality
- [ ] Screenshot showing the graph before and after changes.
- [ ] New or updated unit/integration tests.


### Notes for reviewers

This is an EXPERIMENTAL maturity rule. The threshold of `<= 1` accounts
for the fact that an AWSAccount node itself may have a single
self-referencing resource relationship.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Signed-off-by: Jeremy Chapeau <jeremy@subimage.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(tweety): implement 8 Track A capability handlers (#55-#62)

Add handlers for all 8 Tweety argumentation frameworks:
- #55 RankingHandler: categorizer, burden, discussion, counting, tuples ranking
- #56 ASPICHandler: strict/defeasible rules, simple/directional reasoners
- #57 BeliefRevisionHandler: Dalal, Levi revision + kernel contraction
- #58 ABAHandler: preferred/stable/complete/ideal semantics + file parsing
- #59 ProbabilisticHandler: subgraph enumeration for acceptance probabilities
- #60 BipolarHandler: necessity and evidential frameworks (attack + support)
- #61 ADFHandler: 7 semantics (grounded to stable) + KppADF parsing
- #62 DialogueHandler: grounded-based argumentation dialogue protocol

Register all handlers as services in CapabilityRegistry with invoke functions.
Wire lazy-loaded properties in TweetyBridge for all 8 handlers.
29 new unit tests covering initialization, analysis, and registration.

Instead of just generating more text, Faultline dissects AI answers into atomic claims, verifies them against real-world data using Google Search grounding, and visualizes logical fractures in the reasoning chain.

commit.message:
fix: eliminate SSRF TOCTOU and ReDoS vulnerabilities (#341)

* fix: eliminate SSRF TOCTOU and ReDoS vulnerabilities (closes #340)

**SSRF (CWE-918 — CodeQL alert #28)**
Replace the two-step validate-then-fetch approach in url-fetcher.ts with
IP-pinned requests using node:http / node:https directly. validateUrl()
resolves DNS and checks for private IPs, then the validated IP is passed
straight to the TCP connection (hostname: pinnedIp, servername: original
hostname for TLS SNI). There is now zero TOCTOU window between validation
and the actual network request. The redundant post-fetch DNS rebinding
check and the env-var-based TLS bypass wrapper are removed; rejectUnauthorized
is now passed directly to the request options.

An internal _setRequestImpl hook is exported for unit test injection so
tests can stub responses without touching node:http / node:https.
Tests are updated accordingly.

**ReDoS (CWE-1333 — CodeQL alert #24)**
Five regexes in confluence.ts used the pattern [^>]*ac:name="X"[^>]* —
two [^>]* quantifiers around a fixed literal. For input that contains a
large attribute blob without the target ac:name value, the engine must try
all O(n²) splits before concluding no match (catastrophic backtracking).

Fix: rewrite the leading [^>]* as (?:(?!ac:name="X")[^>])* — a negative
lookahead prevents the quantifier from overlapping with the literal,
making backtracking structurally impossible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace remaining polynomial regexes in confluence.ts with indexOf helpers

The conflict resolution left the original `/<ac:structured-macro [^>]*>([\s\S]*?)
<\/ac:structured-macro>/gi` patterns in place for code blocks and info/tip panels
(those were not part of the original security fix diff). These have the same
O(n²) backtracking problem: with k opening tags and no closing tags, [\s\S]*?
scans O(n - pos) chars per attempt, totalling O(n²).

Replace the entire convertConfluenceStorage function with the indexOf-based
approach (replaceStructuredMacros / replaceTagPairs / extractTagContent helpers)
that eliminates all regex-based tag parsing. Also add removeSelfClosingMacros
to handle the self-closing TOC case without regex, since the previous self-closing
fix still used a [^>]*ac:name="toc"[^>]* pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Funhouse agent: self-contained dispatch layer, no foundry dependency

Replace foundry-based dispatch with 16 internal adapter modules that
route tool calls directly to analysis modules. The funhouse agent now
works standalone via pip install without requiring foundry/ files.

New files:
- funhouse_agent/dispatch.py — tool dispatch routing to adapters
- funhouse_agent/system_prompt.py — self-contained prompt (16 modules)
- funhouse_agent/adapters/ — 16 adapter modules bridging flat JSON
  to structured analysis module APIs

Updated:
- agent.py imports dispatch from local dispatch.py (was chat_agent)
- vision_tools.py defines STANDARD_TOOLS locally (was chat_agent.parser)
- Documentation: guides, DESIGN.md, CLAUDE.md reflect new architecture
- Test count: 42 tests (was 32)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
